user.title: Rover
user.description: Rover will create a Reticulum identity and prepare the mesh transport. You can add devices and manage remotes in the integration options.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one Rover instance is allowed.

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Select which Home Assistant entities Rover should expose to remotes.

users.title: Active remotes
users.description: Approved remotes ({active}/{max}). Remove a remote to free a slot.

pending.title: Pending registration requests
pending.description: {count} request(s) waiting for approval. New requests go here when at least one owner exists.

config.title: Configuration & QR
config.description: Scan this QR code from Rover-App to add this server. Export identity for backup or migration.

level.name: Level
level.description: Log level: debug, info, warning, error.

duration_minutes.name: Duration (minutes)
duration_minutes.description: How long to keep the new level before reverting to defaults.

send_test_message.name: Send test message
send_test_message.description: Send a real LXMF message through the Reticulum stack (use 'self' for loopback).

destination_hash.name: Destination hash
destination_hash.description: Hex string (32 chars) or 'self' for loopback.

payload.name: Payload
payload.description: JSON object — the fields content of the message.

simulate_inbound.name: Simulate inbound message
simulate_inbound.description: Inject a message directly into dispatcher, bypassing transport.

source_hash.name: Source hash
source_hash.description: Hex string (32 chars) — the simulated sender identity.

authorized.name: Authorized
authorized.description: Temporarily treat source_hash as approved for this message.

dump_registry.name: Dump registry
dump_registry.description: Log the current registry state for debugging.
